vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of advanced degradation. The registry is largely invalidated by the absence of visible, healthy plant matter.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: Yellow pot is present but contains no visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No evidence of Mexican Mint.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: The Pothos specimen has vanished; the white rabbit anchor remains but is isolated.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No evidence of Silver Guest.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no growth detected.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface, no growth detected.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present, but the Pothos leaf previously associated with this pot has been removed or decayed.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface, no growth detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dark and potentially saturated, but devoid of biological activity.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells across the soil surface, acting as a non-biological substrate.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no signs of organic matter spillover.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial state shows a single leaf in P3 and a single leaf in P4. P1 and P2 are empty.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Gradual loss of structural integrity in the remaining leaves. P3 leaf shows signs of chlorosis/wilting.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: Total disappearance of all plant specimens. The biome has transitioned from a struggling nursery to a barren state.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. All registered specimens have suffered total mortality or removal. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil amendment that failed to support the primary specimens.
anomalies: Introduction of crushed eggshells as a soil top-dressing.
Complete removal/decay of all plant biomass over the 5-day observation window.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic decline in the botanical health of the desk biome. Starting from a state of minimal occupancy (two leaves across four pots), the system has progressed to total plant loss. The white rabbit anchor, once a companion to a Pothos leaf, now sits in an empty pot. The environment is currently sterile of living plant tissue.
